claim: For x' = r x (1 - x/K), the equilibrium x=K is locally stable when r > 0 and K > 0.
success_criteria: Computes derivative of the RHS with respect to x.
failure_modes: Ignores the sign assumptions on r and K.
Uses only a plot or simulation.
prompt: For the logistic equation x' = r x (1 - x/K), test whether the equilibrium x=K is locally stable under r>0 and K>0. Produce symbolic evidence.
